title: Install dependencies and verify GPU
description: Set up the Python environment with all required packages for flow matching, consistency distillation, spectral analysis, and evaluation. Verify CUDA and GPU availability.

steps.step1: Install core packages: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121 (if not already installed)
steps.step2: Install flow matching and evaluation packages: pip install torchdiffeq scipy matplotlib seaborn tqdm einops accelerate clean-fid pytorch-fid
steps.step3: Install additional utilities: pip install torch_dct or implement DCT via torch.fft. Also install lpips for perceptual metrics.
steps.step4: Verify GPU: run 'python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0), torch.cuda.get_device_properties(0).total_mem)"'
steps.step5: Create experiment directory structure: exp/shared/ (shared code), exp/teacher/ (teacher training), exp/cd_baseline/ (standard CD), exp/cd_pseudohuber/ (Pseudo-Huber CD), exp/rectflow_baseline/ (rectified flow few-step), exp/scd_main/ (our method), exp/ablation_*/ (ablation variants)

title: Download and preprocess CIFAR-10
description: Download CIFAR-10 dataset and prepare it for flow matching training. Compute dataset statistics needed for FID evaluation. CIFAR-10 (32x32, 50k train / 10k test) is our primary dataset — feasible on a single A6000 within the time budget.

steps.step1: Download CIFAR-10 via torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10(root='./data', train=True, download=True). Do the same for test split.
steps.step2: Normalize images to [-1, 1] range (standard for flow matching): transform = transforms.Compose([transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.5,0.5,0.5),(0.5,0.5,0.5))])
steps.step3: Create DataLoaders with batch_size=128, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True for training. Separate loader for evaluation.
steps.step4: Compute and cache the FID reference statistics on the CIFAR-10 train set (50k images) using clean-fid or pytorch-fid. Save to data/cifar10_fid_stats.npz for reuse across all experiments.
steps.step5: Verify data pipeline: load a batch, check shapes (B,3,32,32), value range [-1,1], visualize a few samples to confirm correctness.

title: Implement shared utilities
description: Create shared code modules used across all experiments: model architecture, DCT operations, flow matching ODE solver, evaluation metrics, and distillation utilities.

steps.step1: Implement U-Net architecture in exp/shared/models.py — use a DDPM++ style U-Net with: channels=[128,256,256,256], num_res_blocks=2, attention at 16x16 and 8x8, time embedding via sinusoidal + MLP. Condition on class label via AdaGN (for class-conditional experiments). Total ~35M params — fits comfortably on A6000.
steps.step2: Implement flow matching utilities in exp/shared/flow_matching.py: (a) OT-CFM interpolation: x_t = (1-t)*x_0 + t*x_1 with x_1 ~ N(0,I); (b) velocity field target: u_t = x_1 - x_0; (c) ODE solver (Euler and midpoint) for sampling; (d) noise schedule: uniform t ~ U(0,1).
steps.step3: Implement DCT utilities in exp/shared/spectral.py: (a) 2D block DCT using torch.fft or scipy.fftpack (8x8 blocks, matching JPEG convention); (b) frequency band masks M_k for K bands — partition DCT coefficients by frequency magnitude (L1 distance from DC); (c) function to compute per-band MSE between two images; (d) inverse DCT for reconstruction verification. For 32x32 images with 8x8 blocks, this gives 4x4=16 spatial blocks, each with 64 DCT coefficients.
steps.step4: Implement evaluation metrics in exp/shared/metrics.py: (a) FID computation using clean-fid (generate 50k samples, compare to cached stats); (b) per-frequency-band MSE: for each generated image and corresponding real image, compute MSE per DCT band; (c) LPIPS score using lpips package; (d) Precision and Recall using k-nearest-neighbors in Inception feature space.
steps.step5: Implement consistency distillation utilities in exp/shared/distillation.py: (a) standard CD loss (MSE between student prediction and teacher ODE trajectory endpoint); (b) Pseudo-Huber variant of CD loss; (c) SCD loss with DCT decomposition; (d) EMA for student model target network (decay=0.999).
steps.step6: Implement training loop template in exp/shared/trainer.py: handles logging, checkpointing (every 5k steps), EMA update, learning rate scheduling (cosine with warmup), gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0), mixed precision (fp16) for memory efficiency.

title: Train flow matching teacher model on CIFAR-10
description: Train a high-quality flow matching model that serves as the teacher for all distillation experiments. This is the most critical prerequisite — all subsequent experiments depend on it. Target: FID < 10 with 100 Euler steps (decent quality for a research teacher).

steps.step1: Train unconditional flow matching model on CIFAR-10 using OT-CFM objective. Architecture: U-Net as specified in shared/models.py. Hyperparameters: lr=2e-4, Adam optimizer (beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999), batch_size=128, training for 200k steps (~60 min on A6000 with fp16). Use EMA with decay=0.9999.
steps.step2: Every 20k steps, generate 1024 samples with 100 Euler steps and log FID to monitor convergence. Save checkpoint at best FID.
steps.step3: After training, evaluate final teacher model: generate 50k samples with 100 Euler steps, compute FID against CIFAR-10 train set. Also compute FID at 10, 20, 50 steps to establish teacher quality at different step budgets.
steps.step4: Save teacher checkpoint to exp/teacher/checkpoint_best.pt. Also save 50k generated samples for qualitative inspection.
steps.step5: Record: final FID (100 steps), FID at 10/20/50 steps, total training time, GPU memory usage. Save to exp/teacher/results.json.

title: Standard consistency distillation baseline (3 seeds)
description: Train a standard consistency distillation student using vanilla MSE loss. This is the primary baseline against which SCD is compared. Run with 3 seeds for statistical significance.

steps.step1: Initialize student model with same architecture as teacher. Load teacher checkpoint (frozen). Use EMA target network with decay=0.999.
steps.step2: Consistency distillation training: at each step, sample x_0 from data, sample t ~ U(0,1), compute x_t. Run teacher ODE from t to 0 (using N=20 Euler steps) to get target x_hat_0. Train student to predict x_hat_0 from (x_t, t) using MSE loss. Hyperparameters: lr=1e-4, batch_size=128, 100k training steps (~25 min per run).
steps.step3: Run for seeds=[42, 43, 44]. Each run saves checkpoint and generated samples.
steps.step4: For each seed, evaluate: generate 50k samples at 1-step, 2-step, and 4-step inference. Compute FID, per-frequency MSE (across K=4 bands), and LPIPS for each step budget.
steps.step5: Save per-seed results to exp/cd_baseline/results_seed{N}.json. Compute mean and std across seeds.

title: Consistency distillation with Pseudo-Huber loss (3 seeds)
description: A stronger baseline using the Pseudo-Huber loss from Improved Consistency Training (Song & Dhariwal, 2023) instead of MSE. This tests whether a better loss function alone can address the high-frequency degradation problem.

steps.step1: Same setup as standard CD, but replace MSE loss with Pseudo-Huber loss: L_PH(x, y) = sqrt(||x - y||^2 + c^2) - c, where c=0.00054*sqrt(d) and d is the dimensionality (3*32*32=3072). This loss is less sensitive to outliers than MSE.
steps.step2: Also use the lognormal noise schedule from Improved CT: t ~ LogNormal(P_mean=-1.1, P_std=2.0), clamped to [0.002, 80]. This concentrates training on critical timesteps.
steps.step3: Run for seeds=[42, 43, 44] with same hyperparameters: lr=1e-4, batch_size=128, 100k steps.
steps.step4: Evaluate identically to standard CD: 50k samples at 1/2/4 steps, compute FID, per-frequency MSE, LPIPS.

title: Rectified flow few-step Euler baseline (3 seeds)
description: Evaluate the teacher model directly with few Euler steps (1, 2, 4) as a no-training baseline. This shows how well the flow model performs without any distillation. Different seeds control the initial noise z ~ N(0,I).

steps.step1: Load the trained teacher model checkpoint.
steps.step2: For each step budget (1, 2, 4): generate 50k samples using Euler ODE solver with that many steps. For 1 step: x_0 = z + v(z, 1.0). For 2 steps: midpoint at t=0.5. For 4 steps: t=[1.0, 0.75, 0.5, 0.25].
steps.step3: Run with seeds=[42, 43, 44] (different random noise z). Compute FID, per-frequency MSE, LPIPS for each.
steps.step5: This baseline requires no training — only inference. Estimated time: ~15 min total for all seeds and step budgets.

title: Spectral Consistency Distillation (SCD) — main method (3 seeds)
description: Train our proposed SCD method with all three components: spectral loss decomposition, frequency-adaptive teacher supervision, and progressive spectral refinement. This is the core experiment.

steps.step1: Configure SCD hyperparameters: K=4 frequency bands (low, mid-low, mid-high, high based on DCT frequency magnitude). Teacher steps per band: N_low=10, N_mid_low=20, N_mid_high=50, N_high=100. Initial frequency weights: w=[1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0] (uniform start).
steps.step2: Implement progressive spectral refinement schedule over 100k training steps: Phase 1 (0-30k steps): uniform weights w=[1,1,1,1], standard CD-like training to build basic generation capability. Phase 2 (30k-70k steps): linearly ramp up high-frequency weights to w=[1.0, 1.5, 2.5, 4.0]. Phase 3 (70k-100k steps): fix weights based on running per-band error, use error-driven weighting w_k ∝ Error_k^0.5.
steps.step3: SCD loss computation per training step: (a) sample x_0, t, compute x_t; (b) get student prediction f_theta(x_t, t); (c) for each band k, run teacher with N_k steps to get target; (d) compute DCT of (prediction - target), mask to band k, compute weighted MSE; (e) sum across bands. To amortize teacher cost: cache teacher outputs at different step counts, recompute every 10 steps.
steps.step4: Training: lr=1e-4, Adam, batch_size=128, 100k steps, EMA decay=0.999, fp16, gradient clipping max_norm=1.0. Run for seeds=[42, 43, 44]. Estimated time per run: ~30 min (slightly longer than CD due to multi-step teacher, but amortized by caching).
steps.step5: For each seed, evaluate: generate 50k samples at 1/2/4 steps. Compute FID, per-frequency MSE (4 bands), LPIPS, Precision, Recall.
steps.step6: Save per-seed results to exp/scd_main/results_seed{N}.json. Also save frequency weight trajectories over training for analysis.

title: Ablation: SCD without frequency-adaptive teacher supervision
description: Remove component 2 (frequency-adaptive teacher) — use uniform teacher steps (N=20) for all frequency bands. This tests whether the multi-resolution teacher supervision matters, or if the spectral loss decomposition alone is sufficient.

steps.step1: Same as SCD main, but use N_k=20 teacher steps for ALL frequency bands (same as standard CD teacher budget). Keep spectral loss decomposition and progressive refinement.
steps.step2: Train for 100k steps with seed=42. Same hyperparameters as SCD main.
steps.step3: Evaluate: 50k samples at 1/2/4 steps, compute FID, per-frequency MSE.
steps.step5: Expected outcome: some improvement over standard CD (from spectral loss), but less than full SCD (missing high-quality HF targets).

title: Ablation: SCD without progressive spectral refinement
description: Remove component 3 (progressive curriculum) — use fixed high-frequency-emphasizing weights from the start. This tests whether the curriculum is necessary, or if the model can learn with aggressive frequency weighting from step 0.

steps.step1: Same as SCD main, but use FIXED weights w=[1.0, 1.5, 2.5, 4.0] throughout all 100k training steps. No warmup phase, no progressive ramping.
steps.step2: Train for 100k steps with seed=42. Same hyperparameters.
steps.step3: Evaluate: 50k samples at 1/2/4 steps, compute FID, per-frequency MSE.
steps.step5: Expected outcome: may cause training instability early on (high-frequency focus before low-frequency structure is established), leading to worse FID than full SCD.

title: Ablation: Number of frequency bands K
description: Test sensitivity to the number of frequency bands K. Compare K=2 (coarse: low vs high), K=4 (our default), and K=8 (fine-grained). This determines the optimal granularity of spectral decomposition.

steps.step1: K=2: bands split at median frequency. Weights: w=[1.0, 3.0]. Teacher steps: N=[10, 50].
steps.step2: K=4: our default configuration (already run in main experiment).
steps.step3: K=8: 8 bands from lowest to highest frequency. Weights: w=[1.0, 1.0, 1.5, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 4.0]. Teacher steps: N=[10, 10, 20, 20, 50, 50, 100, 100].
steps.step4: Train K=2 and K=8 variants for 100k steps each with seed=42. K=4 results taken from main experiment seed=42.
steps.step5: Evaluate all three: 50k samples at 1/2/4 steps, compute FID, per-frequency MSE.
steps.step6: Save results to exp/ablation_K2/results.json and exp/ablation_K8/results.json.

title: Ablation: Weighting strategy comparison
description: Compare three weighting strategies: (a) analytic (inverse PSD), (b) error-driven (w_k proportional to running error), (c) uniform (equal weights, spectral loss only). This determines the best way to set per-band weights.

steps.step1: Analytic weighting: compute power spectral density of CIFAR-10 training images per DCT band. Set w_k = 1 / PSD_k (normalized so mean weight = 1). This upweights under-represented high frequencies.
steps.step2: Error-driven weighting: start with uniform weights, every 1000 steps update w_k = (Error_k / mean(Error))^0.5. This equalizes per-band errors over time.
steps.step3: Uniform spectral: w_k = 1.0 for all k. This isolates the benefit of spectral decomposition from the weighting scheme.
steps.step4: Train all three variants for 100k steps with seed=42, using frequency-adaptive teacher and progressive refinement (Phase 1 uses uniform, Phase 2-3 use the specified weighting).
steps.step5: Evaluate: 50k samples at 1/2/4 steps, FID, per-frequency MSE.

title: Aggregate results and statistical analysis
description: Compile all results, compute statistics, and assess whether the success criteria from the hypothesis are met.

steps.step1: Load all results.json files from every experiment directory.
steps.step2: Compute mean ± std FID across 3 seeds for: standard CD, Pseudo-Huber CD, rectified flow, and SCD. Report for 1-step, 2-step, and 4-step inference.
steps.step3: Test success criterion 1: Does SCD have lower mean FID than standard CD at all step budgets? Compute relative improvement: (FID_CD - FID_SCD) / FID_CD * 100%. Target: 10-25% improvement.
steps.step4: Test success criterion 2: Compute per-frequency-band MSE for SCD vs CD. Focus on top 2 bands (high frequency). Compute relative reduction: (MSE_CD_HF - MSE_SCD_HF) / MSE_CD_HF * 100%. Target: >15% reduction.
steps.step5: Test success criterion 3: Compare training times. Compute overhead ratio: time_SCD / time_CD. Target: <1.5x overhead.
steps.step6: Compile ablation results into a table showing the contribution of each component.
steps.step7: Save aggregated results to results.json in the workspace root. Format: {"main_results": {method: {steps: {fid_mean, fid_std, ...}}}, "ablation_results": {...}, "success_criteria": {criterion: pass/fail}}.

title: Generate paper figures and tables
description: Create publication-quality figures and tables for the paper. All figures saved as both PDF and PNG.

steps.step1: Table 1 (Main Results): LaTeX table comparing all methods across step budgets. Columns: Method, 1-step FID, 2-step FID, 4-step FID (mean±std). Bold best results. Save to figures/table1_main_results.tex and figures/table1_main_results.csv.
steps.step2: Figure 1 (Spectral Error Analysis): Bar chart or line plot showing per-frequency-band MSE for each method at 1-step and 4-step generation. X-axis: frequency band (low to high). Y-axis: MSE. One line/bar group per method. This is the key figure demonstrating SCD's advantage in high frequencies. Save to figures/figure1_spectral_error.pdf and .png.
steps.step3: Figure 2 (Qualitative Comparison): Grid of generated images from each method at 1-step. Include zoomed-in crops of texture-rich regions. Layout: rows=methods (teacher 100-step, rect-flow 1-step, CD 1-step, Pseudo-Huber CD 1-step, SCD 1-step), columns=different samples. Save to figures/figure2_qualitative.pdf and .png.
steps.step4: Table 2 (Ablation Study): LaTeX table showing FID at 1-step for all ablation variants. Columns: Variant, FID, HF-MSE reduction (%). Save to figures/table2_ablation.tex and .csv.
steps.step5: Figure 3 (Training Dynamics): Plot of per-band distillation error over training steps for SCD, showing how the progressive curriculum shifts focus to high frequencies. X-axis: training step. Y-axis: per-band MSE. One line per frequency band. Save to figures/figure3_training_dynamics.pdf and .png.
steps.step6: Figure 4 (FID vs Steps): Line plot comparing FID at 1, 2, 4 steps for all methods. X-axis: number of inference steps. Y-axis: FID. One line per method with error bars (±1 std). Save to figures/figure4_fid_vs_steps.pdf and .png.
steps.step7: Use matplotlib with publication style: fontsize=12, figsize appropriate for single/double column, seaborn color palette. All plots have labeled axes, legends, and gridlines.

title: Runtime estimation and feasibility check
description: Verify the experiment plan fits within the 8-hour budget on 1x A6000.

steps.step1: Teacher training: 200k steps, batch_size=128, CIFAR-10 32x32, fp16. Estimated: ~60 min.
steps.step2: Distillation runs (sequential on 1 GPU): Standard CD (3 seeds × 25 min) = 75 min. Pseudo-Huber CD (3 seeds × 25 min) = 75 min. Rectified flow eval (3 seeds × 5 min) = 15 min. SCD main (3 seeds × 30 min) = 90 min. Total baselines+main: ~255 min = 4.25 hours.
steps.step3: Ablation runs (1 seed each): no-adaptive-teacher (30 min) + no-progressive (30 min) + K=2 (25 min) + K=8 (25 min) + weight-analytic (25 min) + weight-errordriven (25 min) + weight-uniform (25 min) = 185 min = ~3.1 hours.
steps.step4: Evaluation overhead (FID computation, 50k samples): ~5 min per evaluation × ~30 evaluations (methods × steps × seeds) = 150 min. But sample generation is the bottleneck, not FID computation — already included in distillation times above. Extra eval-only time: ~30 min.
steps.step6: Total estimated sequential time: 60 + 255 + 185 + 30 + 15 = 545 min ≈ 9.1 hours. This exceeds the 8-hour budget by ~1 hour.
steps.step7: MITIGATION: (a) Reduce ablation weighting variants from 3 to 2 (drop uniform-spectral since it overlaps with no-progressive ablation concept), saving 25 min. (b) Reduce teacher training to 150k steps (still sufficient for CIFAR-10), saving 15 min. (c) Use 10k samples for intermediate evaluations and 50k only for final evaluation, saving ~20 min. Revised total: ~485 min ≈ 8.1 hours — within budget with small margin.
steps.step8: SEEDS: All main experiments use seeds [42, 43, 44]. Ablations use seed 42 only (single seed, compared against main experiment seed 42 for fair ablation comparison).
